text: You fly like the autopilot filed a restraining order against you.
Your clone bay sees more action than your guns ever will.
Your situational awareness has a structure timer, and it just expired.
You have died to the same gate camp so many times they named a shift after you.
Your warp drive works fine; it's the pilot that keeps failing to initialize.
You navigate like the star map personally offended you.
Somewhere a flight simulator uses your gameplay footage as the fail state.
You call it a strategic retreat; the killboard calls it Tuesday.
The enemy logged your shots as friendly fire out of pity.
You would find a way to lose a one versus zero.
Your escape plan is a distress beacon with extra steps.
You warp to zero on everything except the point.
Your fitting looks like you rolled dice on the market and lost.
You treat the overview like optional reading.
Your d-scan button is the least used control in the galaxy.
You are the reason ship insurance premiums exist.
Your kill-to-death ratio needs a support group.
You undock like the station kicked you out for loitering.
Your pod has more frequent flyer miles than your ship.
You align like you are saying goodbye to every asteroid personally.
Command saw your flight record and requested a second opinion.
Your map awareness ends at the undock button.
You could get ambushed in a completely empty system.
The tutorial called; it wants to try you again from the start.
Your reaction time is measured in structure timers.
You broadcast for reps before the fight even starts.
Your best combat maneuver is logging off.
Your ship insurance company has your portrait on a dartboard.
You tackle like you are afraid of commitment.
You bring a mining laser to every fleet fight and somehow it is not the worst part of your fit.
Your bookmarks folder is just the word help written forty times.
Every time you say I am safe, a new wreck appears on my overview.
You are living proof that pods do not come with a skill requirement.
Your capacitor runs dry faster than your excuses.
You once lost a shuttle to a stationary structure.
Your threat assessment is a coin flip, and the coin hates you.
You have jumped into the wrong system so often it counts as your home now.
Fleet command mutes your channel on principle, and honestly, fair.
Your damage output is a rounding error.
You orbit the wrong target with total confidence.
Your loss mails read like performance art.
The gate camp thanks you for your continued business.
You fly expensive ships like you are allergic to keeping them.
Your wingmen filed you under environmental hazards.
You confuse bravery with forgetting to check local.
Your last brilliant plan got the whole fleet a group discount at the clone bay.
You could crash an escape pod in open space.
The autopilot flies better than you, and the autopilot is famous for getting people killed.
You are the only pilot who can lose a ship in the fitting window.
Your trigger discipline died with your first ship.
Your gun tracking is a conspiracy theory.
You read the battle report like it happened to someone else.
Your reputation precedes you; that is why everyone warps off.
You are why the fleet doctrine has a page titled do not do this.
Your idea of recon is dying first and reporting from the clone bay.
You take gate camps personally because they take you regularly.
Your loot fairy testified against you.
You could get lost inside a warp tunnel, and it only goes one way.
Your uptime in a fight is shorter than the undock animation.
You have the battlefield awareness of a station spinner with the monitor off.
Your drone management is a hostage situation.
You alt tab during your own fights.
Every fleet commander you have flown under has developed a new nervous habit.
Your safe spots are famous, mostly to the people hunting you.
You warp like you are billed by the astronomical unit.
Your combat log is just apologies.
You anchor on the FC like a duckling, then wander off at the worst possible moment.
Your overview settings are an act of self-sabotage.
You died on the undock. Twice. In the same minute.
The clone bay staff greet you by name and sigh.
Your strategic depth is a puddle in zero gravity.
You fit a cloak and still get caught, which is almost impressive.
You are why we do a fitting check before every op.
You misclick with the confidence of a fleet commander and the results of a cadet.
Your battle plan is warp in, panic, improvise, apologize.
You once shot the fleet's logistics and called it target practice.
Your escape route always runs through the enemy fleet. On purpose, allegedly.
You handle a structure timer like it is a hot potato.
You fly like your ass is the primary and your brain is in a different region.
Your killboard is a damn crime scene and you are always the victim.
You could not hit a station with a smartbomb, and that is a hell of an achievement.
Get your shit together; even the autopilot is embarrassed for you.
You lost your pod to a rat. A damn belt rat.
Your piloting is so bad the wormhole spat your ass back out.
You are a hell of a pilot, the way a hurricane is a hell of a breeze.
Half the fleet has you set to red just to be safe, and honestly, damn right.
Your warp scrambler has scrambled exactly one thing: your own damn plans.
You dropped a billion isk ship because you forgot to press one damn button.
Whoever taught you to fly deserves an apology, because this shit is clearly self-taught.
You navigate nullsec like a shopping cart with a busted wheel and no damn brakes.
Your ass has been podded home so many times you qualify for a loyalty program.
The fleet warped off without you because you were arguing with your own damn drones.
You turned a free kill into a fair fight and a fair fight into a damn memorial service.
Your target calling is shit; you primaried the wreck.
You could not find the enemy fleet with a map, a scout, and a damn escort.
Your ship is lovely; shame about the pilot inside it.
Your ego has a bigger signature radius than a freighter.
You are two bad decisions away from a career in structure bashing, and you already made one of them.
